title: leucanthemum rohlenae
intro: Leucanthemum rohlenae, also known as Rohlena's daisy, is a rare and endangered flowering plant species native to the Czech Republic. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Leucanthemum rohlenae include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as infusions and tinctures, although its use is largely limited to Czech folk medicine due to its rarity and restricted availability.
